Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (Xi Jinping) is the current Prime Minister of China and the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China. His biography can be analyzed in the following steps:

1. Birth and Childhood (1953-1968)
Birth: August 18, 1953, effectively in the city of Beijing. His father was a Communist Party official and his mother was a teacher.
Childhood and Education: He enrolled at Peking University in chemistry (in 1969). During his education, China’s Cultural Revolution (1966–1976) took place, where he worked in agriculture and worked in various places during the Cultural Revolution.
2. Career and Political Rise (1969-2007)
1969-1975: He worked in agriculture in Shaanxi province and later worked in several factories in Shaanxi province.
1975-1982: He worked in the propaganda department of the Communist Party of China and later worked in Shanxi province.
1982-1985: He became a local official in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
1985-1992: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
1992-1998: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
1998-2002: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
2002-2007: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
3. Political Rise (2007-2013)

Barack Obama:
Barack Obama (Barack Obama) was an American president and constitutional representative, who occupied a special place in the minds of the world as the first African-American president of the United States. His biography is described step by step below:

1. Birth and Early Life (1961-1971)
Birth: Obama was born on August 4, 1961 in the place of Sabut in Kenya and his father Hussein Obama was a political leader of Kenya. He played an important role in Kenya’s independence movement.
Father and Mother: His mother Sarah Obama was an American woman of British origin, who lived in Hawaii. His father came to America from Kenya and married his mother.
Early Life: Obama grew up in Hawaii, where he was born in an African-American lineage. His father returned to Kenya and his mother raised him in Hawaii.
2. Education and Career (1971-1990)
Education: Obama enrolled in Oxford College (1971-1972), then enrolled in Columbia University (1972-1981) where he obtained a doctorate degree in political science.
Career: He worked at Columbia University and later worked as a community organizer in a social organization in Chicago. He played an important role in solving social problems in the city of Chicago.
3. Political Career (1990-2004)
First election as Illinois legislator: In 1996 he was elected as a legislator of the state of Illinois. He worked as a constitutional representative and played an important role in solving local problems.
First election as Illinois legislator: 2004
Dilma Rousseff:
Biography of Dilma Rousseff

Early life and education:
Dilma Rousseff (Dilma Rousseff) was born in 1947 in the city of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. She played an important role in political and social work. She obtained a doctorate degree in law from the University of Rio de Janeiro.

Political career:
Rousseff was a member of Brazil’s Workers’ Party (PT). She began political work in the 1990s. In 1995 she became a member of Brazil’s parliament and later became a member of the Senate. In 2002 she became the Prime Minister of Brazil and later became the first female president of Brazil.

Work as President:
In 2011 Rousseff was elected President of Brazil and was re-elected in 2014. She introduced programs for social resource distribution and economic expansion, especially the Bolsa Família program, which provides financial assistance to poor families. She was known for Brazil’s economic development and social resource distribution.

Removal from Presidency:
In 2016 Rousseff was removed from the presidency by Brazil’s parliament on charges of crime. She was accused of committing crimes in the use of government funds. However, this process was controversial, because the accusations were especially against members of parliament. In 2018 after trial in the Supreme Court, the criminal charges were canceled.
